data.title: Webster Financial stock surges after Q3 earnings meet consensus
data.description:
Webster Financial (NYSE:WBS) stock jumped 11% in Thursday midday trading after Q4 earnings, excluding notable items, came in line with the Wall Street consensus. In addition, the top end of its guidance range for Q4 net interest income was higher than the consensus estimate.

The parent of Webster Bank expects Q4 net interest income (non-FTE) of $500M-$600M vs. Visible Alpha consensus of $584.6M. The outlook assumes that the Federal Reserve cuts its policy rate by 25 basis points at each of the next two meetings.

The company said it restructured its securities portfolio to extend duration and lock in higher yielding bonds. That puts it in a better position to withstand a declining interest rate scenario.

Webster (WBS) expects Q4 noninterest income of $85M-$90M vs. $93.3M in Q3 and Visible Alpha estimate of $91.3M.

Loan growth is expected to increase 1%-1.5% Q/Q, and deposits are anticipated to dip ~1% due to seasonality in public funds.

Q3 adjusted EPS of $1.34, matching the average analyst estimate. The adjusted figure excludes losses from securities repositioning, restructuring costs, and other adjustments. GAAP EPS of $1.10 fell from $1.28 in Q2 and $3.85 in last year's Q3.

Net interest income of $588.9M, beating the Visible Alpha estimate of $583.1M, rose from $572.3M in the prior quarter and $587.1M a year ago. Net interest margin of 3.36% increased from 3.32% in Q2 and slipped from 3.49% in the prior-year period.

Q3 adjusted noninterest income of $93.3M, topping the Visible Alpha estimate of $89.3M, grew from $92.2M in Q2 and $90.4M a year ago.

Its provision for credit losses of $54.0M fell from $59.0M in Q2 and rose from $36.5M a year ago. The ratio of net charge-offs to average loans and leases was 0.27% vs. 0.26% in the previous quarter and 0.23% a year ago.

Adjusted noninterest expense of $328.4M compared with $326.0M in the previous quarter and $301.0M a year ago.

Total loans and leases, at $51.9B as of Sept. 30, 2024, edged up from $51.6M at June 30.

Total deposits of $64.5B at Sept. 30 increased from $62.3B at June 30.

data.title: Iridium Communications' upbeat results and forecast signal shares to go higher
data.description:
Shares of Iridium Communications (NASDAQ:IRDM) soared nearly 19% on Thursday after the company posted a top-and-bottom line beat for its third quarter results and updated its full-year forecast.

The company now expects total service revenue growth of about 5% for 2024, updated from the 4% to 6% range previously forecast. It guided operational EBITDA between $465M and $470M, revised from between $460M and $470M.

The satellite communications company said during the quarter, it had lower operating expenses compared to the prior year period, helped by a decline in depreciation expense tied to the extension of the estimated useful lives of its satellites.

At the end of Q3, the company's total billable subscribers rose 11% year-over year to 2,482,000, led by growth in commercial IoT.

Net income for the three months ended September 30 was $24.4M, compared to a net loss of $1.6M for the same period last year. The company said net income benefitted from the year-over-year increases in service revenue, engineering and support revenue, and equipment sales.

On a per share basis, the McLean, Virginia-based company earned 21 cents, edging past average analyst estimates of 19 cents.

Revenue came in at $212.8M, up nearly 8% from a year ago and ahead of the consensus estimate by $6.63M.

Shares of the company are +13.6% by midday. IRDM is the biggest percentage gainer among Alternative Carriers stocks ranked by Seeking Alpha Quant. Stock is down 14.4% so far this year while the benchmark S&P index rose over 22%.

data.title: Huntington Bancshares issues soft Q4 guidance for NII; Q3 earnings top consensus
data.description:
Huntington Bancshares (NASDAQ:HBAN) stock dipped 2% in Thursday late morning trading after issuing soft Q4 guidance for net interest income growth. Still, the regional lender posted Q3 results that surpassed the Wall Street consensus as sequential loan and deposit growth continued

The Ohio-based company sees Q4 NII flat to up 1% from a year ago, trailing the Visible Alpha consensus of +2.63%.

Noninterest income for Q4 is expected to climb 8%-9% Y/Y, in-line with the Visible Alpha estimate of +8.01%, according to a slide presentation. Noninterest expense growth is anticipated to be about 3%.

It expects Q4 average loans and average deposits each rising 4%-5% from a year earlier.

Q3 EPS of $0.33, topping the $0.30 average analyst estimate, advanced from $0.30 in Q2 and slipped from $0.35 a year ago.

"We drove accelerated loan growth and sustained deposit gathering in the quarter, while actively executing our down-rate action plans," said Chairman, President and CEO Steve Steinour. "We are also pleased with a very strong performance in our value added fees businesses."

Q3 net interest income (fully taxable equivalent) rose to $1.36B, matching the Visible Alpha estimate, from $1.33B in Q2 and retreated from $1.38B a year before. Net interest margin of 2.98% vs. 2.99% in Q2 and 3.20% in Q3 2023.

Adjusted noninterest income ascended to $531M (vs. $523M Visible Alpha consensus) from $500M in Q2 and $476M in Q3 of last year. Noninterest expense of $1.13B compared with $1.12B in Q2 and $1.11B in Q3 2023.

Average loans and leases were $126.4B vs. $123.4B in Q2. Average deposits of $156.5B increased from $153.6B in Q2.

data.title: Kezar stock falls 9% after board rejects Concentra takeover offer
data.description:
Shares of Kezar Life Sciences (NASDAQ:KZR) fell 9% Thursday after the company said its board of directors had unanimously rejected an unsolicited takeover offer from Concentra Biosciences and adopted a stockholders rights plan.

Last week, Concentra offered to acquire all of Kezar’s (KZR) outstanding stock for $1.10 per share in cash, plus a contingent value right that represented the right to receive 80% of the net proceeds from the out-licensing or sale of Kezar's (KZR) programs or intellectual property.

Kezar (KZR) said Thursday that after reviewing the offer with its advisors, its board has determined the proposal “substantially undervalues” the company. It added that the offer implies an equity value for Kezar shareholders that is below the company’s available liquidity and fails to adequately value the potential of its drug candidate zetomipzomib, according to a statement.

Kezar added that in response to the offer and the “rapid” accumulation of 9.9% of Kezar’s stock by Concentra and its affiliates, it has immediately adopted a limited duration stockholders rights plan.

“Kezar continues to experience a significant and ongoing dislocation in the trading price of its common stock which does not reflect its fundamental value,” said Kezar Chairman Graham Cooper, in the statement. “We intend the rights plan to enable all of our stockholders to realize the long-term value of their investment.”

In connection with the shareholders rights plan, Kezar’s board also declared a dividend of one preferred share purchase right for each outstanding share of Kezar common stock, subject to certain restrictions. The dividend is payable to shareholders of record as of Oct. 28.

data.title: Cheniere Energy edges closer to launching Texas LNG export operation with FERC OK
data.description:
Cheniere Energy (NYSE:LNG) moves closer to producing first liquefied natural gas from a Corpus Christi, Texas, expansion project after the Federal Energy Regulatory Commission issued an order Thursday allowing the company to take LNG from the plant to liquid storage facilities and then onto a tanker for export.

Cheniere (LNG) has said it wants to produce first LNG from its Corpus Christi Stage 3 facility by year-end, which will expand U.S. LNG export capacity.

Stage 3 is comprised of seven gas-processing units with an expected capacity of ~10M metric tons/year of LNG.

data.title: Couchbase upgraded to Outperform on new product releases: Wedbush
data.description:
Couchbase (NASDAQ:BASE) was upgraded to Outperform from Neutral as Wedbush expects the company to experience accelerated growth in the quarters ahead due to a launch of new products.

Shares jumped 5% by noon trading on Thursday.

Couchbase offers a NoSQL database to help developers build, deploy and run applications.

"Couchbase estimates their TAM to grow to ~$150B by 2028 with high-performance and scalable modern applications driving the market with AI accelerating further opportunity for high-performance applications as the company is aligned to meet demand for growing DBMS market sub-segments across cloud and on-premise environments," said Wedbush analysts, led by Daniel Ives, in a note Thursday.

"Although still early in terms of its contribution to revenues or ARR, the company noted that it plans to disclose Capella metrics going forward with Capella acting as a 'game changer' for BASE with easy adoption or deployment compared to alternative customer managed offerings while helping reduce adoption friction leading to an acceleration in its net new customer adds," Wedbush added.

Competitor MongoDB (MDB) was down about 3% during Thursday trading.

data.title: Financials and Industrials passed 30% 52-week highs simultaneously
data.description:
Stocks in the financial (NYSEARCA:XLF) and industrial (NYSEARCA:XLI) sectors simultaneously surpassed 30% in their 52-week highs on Monday.

This is the 21st time since 1954, SentimenTrader pointed out on X.com.

According to historical records, the S&P 500 (SP500) posted gains in the subsequent year every time this happened.

Credits to SentimenTrader, X.com

data.title: Avangrid drops as Connecticut plans to review Iberdrola deal
data.description:
• Avangrid (NYSE:AGR) quickly fell 0.6% as Connecticut plans to review the utility's planned sale to Spain's Iberdrola (OTCPK:IBDSF).
  • The review was disclosed in a Connecticut utilities filing on Thursday.
  • Developing story ...

data.title: Procter & Gamble Q1 earnings on deck: What to expect
data.description:
Procter & Gamble (NYSE:PG) is expected to release first quarter results on Friday, before markets open.

Wall Street expects the Ohio-based consumer goods company to post EPS of $1.90 on revenue of $21.98 billion during the quarter.

The company, which is considered a consumer sector bellwether, has been facing hardships in the U.S. and China markets, due to weak consumption and a volatile economy. Earlier in July, PG posted a mixed FQ4 earnings report, with organic sales rising just 2% during the quarter to miss the consensus estimate of +3.4%.

The company’s organic sales in China declined by 8% during the fourth quarter, with the company expecting that the recovery would be slow.

“With the North America market growth slowing by a point and China/ Middle East still seeing challenges, we would anticipate organic growth to continue to slow sequentially in 1Q after exiting FY24 with +2.6% in 4Q,” said UBS analyst Peter Grom.

Grom believes investors are braced for organic sales to fall below consensus during the quarter.

“PG has missed topline estimates for three straight quarters now, and the weak organic growth trend may not necessarily reverse in Q1 given a difficult base,” noted a recent Seeking Alpha analysis by The Alpha Sieve.

Over the last two years, PG has beaten EPS estimates 88% of the time and has beaten revenue estimates 63% of the time.

Over the last three months, EPS estimates have seen three upward revisions, compared to 13 downward revisions, while revenue estimates have seen no upward revisions versus 11 downward moves.

Seeking Alpha analysts and Seeking Alpha’s Quant rating are cautious and rated the stock a Hold, while Wall Street analysts consider it a Buy.

Procter & Gamble, which makes Gillette razors, Pampers diapers and Charmin toilet paper, has gained over 17% so far this year.

data.title: Italy in talks with Westinghouse, others for nuclear plants, energy minister says
data.description:
Cameco (NYSE:CCJ) +1.5% in Thursday's trading to an all-time intraday high of $57.45, as Italy's government said it is in talks with several companies including Westinghouse and EDF as potential partners for a state-backed company that will build advanced nuclear reactors.

The government plans to draft rules by year-end allowing the use of new nuclear technologies and lift a ban on nuclear-fired power plants, Energy Minister Gilberto Pichetto Fratin told Reuters.

"We talk to all" the major operators, the minister said, confirming talks specifically with France's EDF and Westinghouse, which is owned by Cameco (CCJ) and Brookfield Renewable Partners (BEP).

The administration led by Prime Minister Meloni has said small modular reactors and advanced modular reactors could help decarbonize Italy's most polluting industries.

ETFs: (URA), (NLR), (URNM)
